ego: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Eggon.
eth: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ethiopian Sign Language.
ethology: noun. The scientific study of animalian behavior, especially that of nonhuman animals.
ethyl: noun. The univalent hydrocarbon radical, C 2 H 5, formally derived from ethane by the loss of a hydrogen atom.
gel: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Fakkanci.
gey: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Enya.
gley: noun. A type of hydric soil, sticky, greenish-blue-grey in colour and low in oxygen.

hey: interjection. An exclamation to get attention.

hole: noun. A hollow place or cavity; an excavation; a pit; a dent; a depression; a fissure.

holy: adjective. Dedicated to a religious purpose or a god.

hoot: noun. A derisive cry or shout.
hooty: adjective. Characterised by a hooting sound.
hot: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Hote.

logo: noun. A visual symbol or emblem that acts as a trademark or a means of identification of a company or organization.
logy: adjective. Slow to respond or react; lethargic.

loot: noun. Synonym of booty, goods seized from an enemy by violence, particularly during the sacking of a town in war or after successful combat.
lot: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lotuko.
lye: noun. An alkaline liquid made by leaching ashes (usually wood ashes).
ogle: verb. To stare at (someone or something), especially impertinently, amorously, or covetously.
oho: interjection. Expressing surprise or gloating realisation; aha.
ole: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Olekha.

ology: noun. Any branch of learning, especially one ending in “-logy”.

tel: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Telugu.
the: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Chitwania Tharu.
theology: noun. The study of God, a god, or gods; and of the truthfulness of religion in general.
they: pronoun. A group of entities previously mentioned.
thy: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tha.
toe: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Tomedes.
tog: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tonga (Malawi).
tole: noun. A decorative metalware having a lacquered or enamelled surface that is painted or gilded.
too: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Xicotepec de Juárez Totonac.
tool: noun. Any physical device meant to ease or do a task.
toy: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Topoiyo.
tye: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kyenga.
yeh: interjection. Alternative form of yeah; yes.
yet: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Yetfa.
